# Telehealth accreditation

### In this guide

## Breadcrumb

## Introduction to telehealth accreditation

Telehealth accreditation helps ensure that telehealth programs adhere to standards, evidence-based best practices, and regulations.

Accreditation programs for in-person health care now include telehealth services. The accreditation process aims to improve virtual care services through review of telehealth programs to meet and maintain established standards. These formal reviews have many benefits. They provide a recognized marker for high quality of care and consistency. This promotes trust and confidence among patients and providers.

Starting and implementing the telehealth accreditation process can be complicated. Additionally, the process may have costs and resource requirements. This best practice guide describes how telehealth accreditation can help health care organizations and providers achieve their goals and support responsible telehealth growth.

## [Getting started](/providers/best-practice-guides/telehealth-accreditation/getting-started)

# Telehealth accreditation

### In this guide

## Breadcrumb

## Advantages of telehealth accreditation

Telehealth accreditation verifies that the delivery of virtual care is consistent and follows accepted standards.

## On this page:

## Adherence to standards for telehealth

A major aim of accreditation is to create trust and confidence in telehealth. Telehealth accreditation facilitates the alignment and enhancement of telehealth programs by evaluating them against recognized, consistent standards. Examples include:

Incorporating these standards into a telehealth program and becoming accredited demonstrates a commitment to excellence.

## Promotion of trust and confidence in telehealth

Accreditation helps promote credibility and trust for telehealth services in the eyes of patients, providers, and payers. Accreditation demonstrates that a telehealth program has achieved a set of standards in the delivery of virtual care and is committed to continuous improvement.

Here are several ways that accreditation encourages trust and confidence in using telehealth:

The accreditation process is essential for fostering trust, driving continuous improvement, and ensuring the responsible growth of the telehealth industry.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Accreditation ensures telehealth programs meet established standards, fostering trust and enhancing quality of care, thereby promoting responsible telehealth growth and patient confidence.
The Joint Commission and URAC are leading accrediting organizations that establish rigorous standards for telehealth services, ensuring compliance with industry best practices and regulations.
Accreditation involves comprehensive evaluations and surveys of telehealth programs, assessing adherence to clinical, technical, and operational standards to ensure consistent quality of service.
Accreditation typically lasts three years, requiring periodic renewal through re-evaluations to maintain standards and adapt to evolving telehealth practices and regulations.
